design_review_system: docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/design/review-by-system-designer.md (Praxis PASS + 3 additions encoded as Slice-4 scenarios)
scaffold_root: tests/acceptance/frontend-coexistence/ (pytest + httpx + subprocess; standalone pyproject.toml + venv per CLAUDE.md acceptance-suite convention)
walking_skeleton_strategy: C — real local + skip-when-unavailable (per DI-1; mirrors `tests/acceptance/ibis-as-only-sql-compiler/` Strategy-C pattern)
test_framework: pytest + httpx + subprocess (no pytest-bdd, no Playwright at MR-0; DELIVER may add playwright-python when un-skipping the DOM-fingerprint scenario per DI-2)

phases.title: Phase 01 — Slice 1 / MR-0: RRv7 framework-mode plumbing (zero behavior change)
phases.scope: Land the RRv7 framework-mode substrate as one atomic MR per ADR-034 §"Migration sequence" row 1. New files: `frontend/app/root.tsx`, `frontend/app/routes.ts`, `frontend/app/lib/ui-state-client.ts`, `frontend/ssr.ts`. `git mv` the five scaffold files from `ui-presentation/app/routes/` to `frontend/app/routes/`. DELETE `frontend/App.tsx`. REWRITE `frontend/main.tsx` to the RRv7 `<HydratedRouter />` hydration entry. Update `frontend/vite.config.ts` (add `reactRouter()` plugin AND remove `@vitejs/plugin-react` — load-bearing per DESIGN F-2 inline resolution). Update `frontend/tsconfig.json` to include `app/**/*`. Update `frontend/src/ui/components/AppShell/index.tsx` to move RequireAuth/RequireOrg guards in. DELETE `ui-presentation/` directory (including its package.json, tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts, package-lock.json). REMOVE `ui-presentation` from root `package.json` workspaces. System-level: add `:ssr_dist` + `:ssr_image` + `:ssr_image_tar` to `frontend/BUILD.bazel`; modify `frontend/nginx.conf` to proxy_pass catch-all to `web-ssr:3001`; insert `web-ssr:` block in `docker-compose.yml`. Walking-skeleton scenario unpends; §3.1, §3.5, §3.7, §3.8 scenarios unpend.

phases.title: Phase 02 — Slice 2 / MR-1: first per-route migration (loader + dehydratedState + AppShell inner QueryProvider removal)
phases.scope: Migrate one route to framework mode by adding a server `loader` that calls `uiStateClient(request).getProjection(...)` and returns `{ dehydratedState: dehydrate(client) }`. Likely target is `/login` per DESIGN's worked example (`application-architecture.md` §2 migration playbook) — DELIVER may pick a different route based on product priority; whatever DELIVER picks becomes `MIGRATED_ROUTE_PATH` for the acceptance suite. The route component wraps its tree in `<HydrationBoundary state={dehydratedState}>`. After this MR lands, `AppShell` no longer wraps children in `<QueryProvider>` (DWD-7); the module-scoped `queryClient` export at `frontend/src/ui/providers/QueryProvider.tsx` is deleted or made a no-op. The auth-proxy test-mirror endpoint (for DI-2's bearer-forwarding assertion) is wired in this phase if it is not already present.
scenarios_to_unskip: docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/migrated-route-renders-html-server-side.feature :: The SSR response contains the server-rendered component output
docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/migrated-route-renders-html-server-side.feature :: The browser hydrates the loader's prefetched data into its singleton QueryClient without re-fetching
docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/migrated-route-renders-html-server-side.feature :: A loader-thrown Response surfaces as that response's status

docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/loader-forwards-bearer-to-auth-proxy.feature :: No loader function references AuthProvider as a value used inside the loader body
docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/loader-forwards-bearer-to-auth-proxy.feature :: No loader function calls `useAuth()` or `useContext()`
docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/loader-forwards-bearer-to-auth-proxy.feature :: AuthProvider's render path produces no error during SSR (every route)
docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/loader-forwards-bearer-to-auth-proxy.feature :: After hydration the client AuthProvider reads sessionStorage and exposes the user identity
docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/loader-forwards-bearer-to-auth-proxy.feature :: Two concurrent SSR requests with different probe bearers do not leak across responses

Optional: `frontend/.eslintrc.*` config for the no-loader-with-chat-import rule (deferred). Total ~50-100 LoC.

phases.title: Phase 03 — Slice 3 / MR-2: reversibility + chat-bearing clientLoader-only opt-out
phases.scope: Two related changes in one atomic MR. (a) Revert the Slice-2 migrated route back to library-mode by removing its `loader` export — keeping the route module file as a pure component. The component file must be byte-identical to its pre-Slice-2 state (the only diff across migrate+revert is the `loader` export added then removed). The route reverts to library-mode behavior: the SSR response is an HTML shell with no pre-rendered loader data; the route fetches its data client-side after hydration. (b) Migrate one chat-bearing route family (e.g., the `/chat/:channelId` route under `<AppShell>`) into framework mode by exporting a `clientLoader` only — NO server `loader`. The ADR-015 `/api/channels/:id/presentation-state` nginx rule remains byte-unchanged. After this phase, the suite has live evidence of both reversibility (forward + reverse) and the DWD-3 clientLoader-only opt-out.

phases.title: Phase 04 — Slice 4 / MR-3: operational readiness (Praxis review §5 additions)
phases.scope: Three operational-readiness invariants validated end-to-end. (a) Loader timeout handling: configure `web-ssr`'s fetch defaults (Hono fetch options or RRv7 handler `getLoadContext` wrapping) so loaders that wait on a slow upstream surface a 500/504 within ≤ 5 seconds wall-clock. Provide a slow-upstream induction mechanism (either an auth-proxy `SLOW_MODE` env toggle or a compose-level `tc qdisc netem delay` shim) so the acceptance test can flip the precondition deterministically. (b) Horizontal scale: bring the compose stack up with `--scale web-ssr=2`; assert two sequential requests to the same (route, bearer) produce byte-equivalent HTML; assert distinct bearers do not leak across instances. (c) Auth-proxy fan-out bound: measure the pre-MR-0 baseline auth-proxy QPS under a representative request mix; replay against a 50% framework-mode migration profile; assert the post-migration QPS is ≤ 110% of the baseline. Record the baseline under `docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/deliver/baseline-metrics.md`.
scenarios_to_unskip: docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/loader-fails-fast-when-auth-proxy-slow.feature :: A loader-backed route responds with 5xx within 5 seconds when auth-proxy is slow
docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/loader-fails-fast-when-auth-proxy-slow.feature :: The timeout-derived error response is rendered through the route's ErrorBoundary, not a stack trace
docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/ssr-instances-produce-identical-html.feature :: Two sequential requests to the same (route, bearer) produce byte-equivalent HTML under --scale web-ssr=2
docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/ssr-instances-produce-identical-html.feature :: Distinct bearers' SSR responses do not contaminate each other across instances
docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/loader-fanout-to-auth-proxy-stays-bounded.feature :: A 50% framework-mode migration profile keeps auth-proxy QPS within 10% of the pre-MR-0 baseline
docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/distill/loader-fanout-to-auth-proxy-stays-bounded.feature :: The pre-MR-0 baseline auth-proxy QPS is recorded under deliver/baseline-metrics.md
phases.files_changed_estimate: frontend/ssr.ts (loader-timeout configuration on the createRequestHandler getLoadContext or via a Hono middleware that wraps fetch with AbortController, ~20L), auth-proxy/index.ts or compose-level shim (slow-upstream induction, ~30L; the env-toggle approach is cleaner — auth-proxy checks `process.env.SLOW_MODE_DELAY_MS` and `setTimeout`s before responding), docker-compose.yml (no change, but the test harness uses `--scale web-ssr=2`), tests/acceptance/frontend-coexistence/conftest.py (add `requires_web_ssr_scale_2` fixture that brings the stack up with `--scale web-ssr=2` and tears it down at session end, ~40L), docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/deliver/baseline-metrics.md (new — recorded auth-proxy QPS baseline + measurement methodology, ~50L). Total ~120-180 LoC.
exit_criteria: All Phase 04 `scenarios_to_unskip` (6 scenarios) are GREEN against the local compose stack

`docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/deliver/baseline-metrics.md` exists and records: (a) the pre-MR-0 auth-proxy baseline QPS measurement with a unit (e.g., `42 QPS over 60s`), (b) the measurement methodology (which routes were hit, what workload generator, total request count, time window), (c) the post-50%-migration measurement and the calculated delta vs baseline, (d) a clear PASS/FAIL line stating the delta is within the 10% ceiling
Slow-upstream induction is documented in `tests/acceptance/frontend-coexistence/README.md` (how to enable it locally)
Browser smoke test: under `--scale web-ssr=2`, opening the migrated route works (no visible quirks)

Reviewer (nw-software-crafter-reviewer + nw-platform-architect-reviewer) approves: bounded-timeout pattern is correctly implemented (no leaked AbortControllers, no orphaned promises), horizontal-scale property is preserved (request-scoped state confirmed)

notes_for_deliver: Iron Rule: never modify a failing test to make it pass. After 3 failed attempts on a step, revert and escalate via clear failure output. See CLAUDE.md `tdd` skill mandate.
Outside-In TDD: write/update the acceptance test (un-skip + run RED) first; drive a unit test from the smallest failing step; make it green with minimum code; refactor per RPP L1–L6.

Trunk-based: every MR lands via `gt mq submit --branch <feature-branch>` from the rig workspace `/home/node/gt/dashboard_chat`. The refinery's `--auto` gate runs `./tools/test/test.sh --auto`. DO NOT use `gh pr create`.

Each phase's `scenarios_to_unskip` lists the scenario titles verbatim as they appear in the .feature file. DELIVER removes the `pytest.mark.skip(...)` from the corresponding test functions in `tests/acceptance/frontend-coexistence/test_*.py` before running the suite.

The acceptance suite is invoked via `cd tests/acceptance/frontend-coexistence && uv run --no-project pytest`. The `--auto` gate's fall-through (`--backend`) does NOT run this suite; the agent or human runs it locally before submitting.

When a phase reveals an upstream gap (a binding decision from DESIGN that doesn't fit the implementation reality, or a missing dependency the design didn't anticipate), append a finding to `docs/feature/frontend-coexistence/deliver/upstream-issues.md` and surface it in the MR description. DO NOT silently modify DESIGN artifacts.
